zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre於1123年出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲巴德 可茲納賀斯朋海姆時，她的父親Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien21歲，母親Mathilde von Sulzbach19歲。她於1140年與Guillaume de Nevers結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1178年5月25日在法國布格奧享年55歲，埋葬在法國布格-福朗-可特艾奧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre于1123年出生在德国海藍-普法爾茲巴德 可茲納賀斯朋海姆时，她的父亲Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien21岁，母亲Mathilde von Sulzbach19岁。她于1140年与Guillaume de Nevers结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1178年5月25日在法国布格奧享年55岁，埋葬在法国布格-福朗-可特艾奧。

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Nevers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from the city of Nevers in France.
Possibly an altered form of English Nevard or perhaps of its extinct variant Neverd. The surname Nevers is also found in Jamaica.

History: The majority of the American bearers of this surname trace their origin to Richard Nevers, born in 1666 in Woburn, MA. His origin is not known, but his surname is also documented in the forms Nevard and Neverds, which make it possible that Richard Nevers was of English origin.

variants.name: Nevard
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Nevard, from ancient Germanic Niwhard, from niw- ‘new’ + hard- ‘strong, hardy’. Compare Nevers .

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heang, Heng, Chheng, Eng, Kheng, Khun, Sang, Seng, Sung.  Vietnamese Anh, Hoa, Huong, Mang, Thao, Thong.  Spanish Angelita, Arturo, Enrique, Guillermo, Renato, Rosario.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 戴, see   Dai   1.
Vietnamese (Tề): from the Chinese surname 齊, see Qi 1.

variants.name: Dai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Cheng, Chi, Jin, Weimin, Xing, Zheng, Chung, Dong, Hong, Meng, Ning, Peihua.  Vietnamese Hung, Quang, Thanh, Anh, Cuong, Dai, Khanh, Lam Van, Minh, Mui, Nghia, Ngoc.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 戴, meaning ‘respect, support’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (戴), the posthumous title of Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). (ii) from Dai (戴), the name of an ancient state (located in Henan province). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng in 713  BC , the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, Yin (殷), because they shared a similar meaning, ‘deep feeling’, in ancient Chinese.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 代, meaning ‘historical periods’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (代), the name of an ancient state (located in Hebei province). After it was annexed by the state of Zhao during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) traced back to the Dai (代) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heang, Heng, Chheng, Eng, Kheng, Khun, Sang, Seng, Sung.  Vietnamese Anh, Hoa, Huong, Mang, Thao, Thong.  Spanish Angelita, Arturo, Enrique, Guillermo, Renato, Rosario.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 戴, see   Dai   1.
Vietnamese (Tề): from the Chinese surname 齊, see Qi 1.

variants.name: Dai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Cheng, Chi, Jin, Weimin, Xing, Zheng, Chung, Dong, Hong, Meng, Ning, Peihua.  Vietnamese Hung, Quang, Thanh, Anh, Cuong, Dai, Khanh, Lam Van, Minh, Mui, Nghia, Ngoc.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 戴, meaning ‘respect, support’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (戴), the posthumous title of Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). (ii) from Dai (戴), the name of an ancient state (located in Henan province). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng in 713  BC , the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, Yin (殷), because they shared a similar meaning, ‘deep feeling’, in ancient Chinese.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 代, meaning ‘historical periods’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (代), the name of an ancient state (located in Hebei province). After it was annexed by the state of Zhao during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) traced back to the Dai (代) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre was born in 1123, in Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, her father, Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien, was 21 and her mother, Mathilde von Sulzbach, was 19. She married Guillaume de Nevers in 1140. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 25 May 1178, in Auxerre, Yonne, Burgundy, France, at the age of 55, and was buried in Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre est née en 1123, à Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, son père, Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mathilde von Sulzbach, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Guillaume de Nevers en 1140. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 mai 1178, à Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne, France, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre 1123 in Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien 21 und ihre Mutter Mathilde von Sulzbach 19. Sie hat 1140 Guillaume de Nevers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. Mai 1178 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Auxerre, Yonne, Burgund, Frankreich und wurde in Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre è nata il nel 1123 nel Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania, sua padre, Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Mathilde von Sulzbach, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Guillaume de Nevers nel 1140. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 25 maggio 1178, nel Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne, Francia, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerreが1123年にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズバド クレウズナチスプオンヘイムで生まれた時，父Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszienは21歳，母Mathilde von Sulzbachは19歳であった。1140年にGuillaume de Neversと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1178年5月25日にフランスボウルゴグンイオンアウクセアで55歳で死去し，フランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeイオンアウクセアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre(이)가 1123년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 바드 크레우즈나치 스폰하임에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilde von Sulzbach(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1140년 Guillaume de Nevers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1178년 5월 25일 프랑스 부르고그니 욘 오세르에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 욘 오세르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre nasceu em 1123, em Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha, seu pai, Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde von Sulzbach, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Guillaume de Nevers em 1140. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de maio de 1178, em Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne, França, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre родилась в 1123 г. в местности споним, бад креузнач, раинланд-пфалз, Германия, её отцу по имени Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mathilde von Sulzbach, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume de Nevers в 1140 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 25 мая 1178 г. в местности ауксирр, енн, бургогн, Франция в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности ауксирр, енн, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ida Von Sponheim, Comtess de Nevers et d'Auxerre et Tonnerre nació en 1123, en Sponheim, Bad Kreuznach, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania, su papá, Markgraf Engelbert III von Spanheim von Istrien von Tuszien, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mathilde von Sulzbach, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Guillaume de Nevers en 1140. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 25 de mayo de 1178, en Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne, Francia, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Auxerre, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.
